items.title: A century ago, a Liberal Party disintegrated, and there are lessons for its namesake

children.content: Last week's South Australian election has left the Liberals in the grip of what one analyst described as an "existential crisis". As the Liberals seek to learn the lessons of the past, they should bear in mind the example of their once mighty namesakes, who ruled Britain for decades before their party turned to dust.

gallery.altText: A painting depicting a crowd in a street

thumbnail.altText: A painting depicting a crowd in a street

items.title: Cotton and Davis involved in post-match spat after 36ers level NBL finals

children.content: Sydney coach Brian Goorjian insists he's not concerned by Kendric Davis's post-match tirade at Adelaide superstar Bryce Cotton, following the 36ers' NBL championship-levelling 91-89 win over the Kings.

image.altText: Tempers flare in the last quarter between Bryce Cotton of the Adelaide 36ers and Kendric Davis of the Sydney Kings

items.title: Bush artist 'Pro' Hart remembered 20 years after death

children.content: On March 28, 2006, Broken Hill lost one of its most renowned residents in internationally acclaimed artist Kevin "Pro" Hart. He was, and still is, considered a widely popular artist, but his recognition was divisive to different audiences.

image.altText: A man in a paint splattered blue shirt and glasses holding a paint brush and a paint board next to a painting of masked figures

items.title: Liberals to retain SA opposition status despite One Nation's second seat, ABC projects

children.content: The ABC is projecting that the Liberals have retained the Adelaide Hills seat of Heysen, meaning the party will be the second largest in the House of Assembly and retain opposition status.

gallery.altText: Man in suit speaking to camera.

thumbnail.altText: Man in suit speaking to camera.

items.title: Fans rethink driving to SA for AFL Gather Round as global fuel crisis continues

children.content: The SA government and AFL are in discussions to try to keep fans flocking to Adelaide for next month's AFL Gather Round, as the global fuel crisis threatens to disrupt travel plans.

image.altText: Multiple yellow signs reading 'pump not in use' attached to petrol pumps at a station

items.title: Hatchet attack on dog at SA golf course prompts public appeal

children.content: A kelpie that was found with "horrific" head wounds at an Adelaide Hills golf course has prompted the RSPCA to issue a public appeal for information.
Warning: This story contains details some readers may find distressing.

image.altText: A dog with stitches in its head.

items.title: 'There is still hope': Former detective reflects six months on from Gus's disappearance

children.content: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

gallery.altText: Composite image of missing boy Gus Lamont imposed on an image of the vast outback with a group of police.

thumbnail.altText: Composite image of missing boy Gus Lamont imposed on an image of the vast outback with a group of police.

items.title: AFL admits crucial umpire error late in Crows' defeat to Cats

children.content: Geelong's Tom Atkins convinced umpires a free-kick was his in a decision the AFL has since clarified should have gone Adelaide's way in a tight contest.

image.altText: Tom Atkins and Zac Taylor contest a ball on the ground near their feet

items.title: Victoria's top order wobbles as South Australia misses big chance in Shield final

children.content: Victoria and South Australia meet in the final of the Sheffield Shield at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

image.altText: Campbell Kellaway walks off the field as South Australian players celebrate the wicket

items.title: Adrian Portelli's company found guilty of unlawful lotteries by SA court

children.content: Melbourne investor Adrian Portelli has been found not guilty of illegal lottery charges but his company has been ordered to pay tens of thousands of dollars in fines.

image.altText: A man holding 100 dollar bills in his hand

items.title: More than 250 voters in Coober Pedy unable to have their say in SA election

children.content: Nearly one in three Coober Pedy voters, or more than 250 people, missed their chance to vote in the state election last week after the town was left without a polling booth on election day.

image.altText: An aerial view of the township of Coober Pedy

items.title: Premier says regional SA will be first in line for fuel amid global crisis

children.content: South Australia's premier has assured farmers battling with soaring diesel prices that regional areas will be first to receive fuel deliveries.

image.altText: The silhouette of a man wearing a trucker cap standing next to a petrol bowser with a pump in hand.

items.title: Why isn't One Nation winning many seats in the SA election despite its high vote?

children.content: One Nation has received more than 20 per cent of the primary vote in South Australia, and so far, its lower house seat count remains slight. Why is that, and why are onlookers so interested in the result?

image.altText: A person voting on a sample House of Representatives ballot paper, with names like Apple and Peach

items.title: 'My left foot was in his mouth': Fisher's lucky escape from 3.5m shark

children.content: A fisher known as "Lucky Luke" to his mates lives up to his nickname after surviving a great white shark dragging him along the ocean floor while lobster fishing off the South Australian coast.

image.altText: A man a black and red t-shirt holds two rock lobsters in front of a boat

items.title: Ryswyk says reaction to coming out has been 'overwhelming'

children.content: Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk says the support he received after becoming the first AFL player to come out as gay has "blown me away".

items.title: Man dubbed 'miracle baby' when he was born dies aged 33

children.content: Ryan Bowman fought through congenital heart defects and cancer, but his family says his final hospital visit led to some frightening final hours.

gallery.altText: A man and woman holding a photo of their son.

thumbnail.altText: A man and woman holding a photo of their son.

items.title: For Australia, the end of the Iran war might just be the start

children.content: The federal government is turning its mind to what Australian support in the Middle East might look like after a ceasefire.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands before a flag.

items.title: Ranked number one on the terrorism index, this nation struck back

children.content: As global attention remains fixed on the sprawling war in the Middle East, another conflict is edging towards a dangerous threshold — one between nuclear-armed Pakistan and Taliban-controlled Afghanistan.

image.altText: A Taliban soldier operates an anti-aircraft gun on a clear day.

items.title: Do we actually have the workforce for the $4b NDIS alternative?

children.content: Months away from the start of a huge shift in disability support for children, the workforce expected to deliver it has concerns about its readiness.

image.altText: An over the shoulder photo of a young girl's hands playing with putty

items.title: Dementia is Australia's top killer, yet most still miss the early signs

children.content: Dementia is the leading cause of death in Australia and stigmas around the disease can delay diagnosis. But some in the community are at work to break taboos.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a hand resting on an elderly woman's hands.

items.title: This man went viral after surviving Bondi. Then the internet took a dark turn

image.altText: A middle-aged man with a white bandage on his head. Telegram messages and images fade in behind him.

children.content: Arsen Ostrovsky was on the ground, bleeding, at the Bondi attack when he decided to take a selfie. Within 2 hours it was circulating on the internet. In less than a day, it had become fodder for a global conspiracy theory.

items.title: 'Built on deception': How one online travel site is fleecing customers

children.content: As holiday-makers gear up for Easter, online travel site Guest Reservations is accused of fleecing customers using hidden fees and charges with advocates calling on government and the regulator to step in.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a suit pulls a suitcase through an airport.

items.title: Head of TGA speaks out on sunscreen scandal

children.content: In his first interview since the sunscreen controversy erupted almost 10 months ago, the Therapeutic Goods Administration head says he hopes the proposed shake-up of regulations will restore consumer confidence.

image.altText: Two tubes of sunscreen with 50+ SPF showing.

items.title: Is government intervention keeping LNG exporters on their 'best behaviour'?

children.content: With war in the Middle East throwing energy markets into chaos, Australian gas prices are strikingly subdued. Experts say looming intervention by Canberra may explain why.

image.altText: A ship docked in the ocean near Darwin.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of 80s punk rock, Harry Potter spoilers and that internet dress

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

items.title: Blueberry industry under scrutiny amid rapid growth

children.content: Australians are eating and growing more blueberries than ever before, but questions are being asked about how production of the fruit is being regulated.
